name: sensible-utils
title: sensible-utils
description:
Utilities for sensible alternative selection
This package provides a number of small utilities which are used
by programs to sensibly select and spawn an appropriate browser,
editor, pager, or terminal emulator.
.
The specific utilities included are: sensible-browser sensible-editor
sensible-pager sensible-terminal

commands.name: select-editor
commands.description: select your default sensible-editor from all installed editors

arguments.description: itor on a per-user basis. It lists the available editors on a system and interactively
arguments.raw: -ed  itor on a per-user basis. It lists the available editors on a system and interactively

arguments.description: select your default sensible-editor from all installed editors
arguments.raw: -editor  select your default sensible-editor from all installed editors

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use select-editor because Utilities for sensible alternative selection.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use select-editor as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `select-editor` as documented upstream for select-editor.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair select-editor with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `select-editor --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.description: itor on a per-user basis. It lists the available editors on a system and interactively

arguments.name: -editor
arguments.description: select your default sensible-editor from all installed editors
